message.content: Adversaries may exfiltrate data using alternative network mediums distinct from the primary command and control channels, such as WiFi, modem, cellular data, Bluetooth, or other RF channels. This technique leverages less-secured or less-monitored connections, often bypassing enterprise defenses that focus on wired Internet traffic. The potential impact includes unauthorized data transfer and information leakage, which can compromise sensitive information. Common exploitation methods involve establishing proximity to the target network and utilizing available wireless or RF channels to transmit data covertly. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and securing all network mediums, implementing robust network segmentation, and employing comprehensive network traffic analysis tools to detect anomalies across different connection types. Regular audits and updates to security policies can further reduce the risk of such exfiltration attempts.
